title: oxypetalum insigne var. nitidum
intro: Oxypetalum insigne var. nitidum, also known as the 'Night-blooming Honeysuckle', is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids. The medicinal properties of Oxypetalum insigne var. nitidum are said to offer various health benefits, including pain relief, wound healing, and antiviral activity. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
